description_long: This radiator features a rectangular, flat white panel with a prominent serpentine metal pipe arrangement mounted on its front surface. The pipes are arranged in a tight, horizontal zigzag pattern, connected by vertical risers at one end, and are secured with visible brackets. The main body appears to be made of metal, likely steel or aluminum, with a powder-coated white finish, while the pipes themselves are metallic with some copper or brass elements at the connection points. A small valve or control knob is visible at the lower end of the pipework, suggesting manual operation. The back of the radiator is a solid, unadorned panel, indicating it is designed for wall mounting. The overall design is utilitarian, with exposed pipework for efficient heat dissipation and easy maintenance.
description: A wall-mounted radiator with a white rectangular panel and exposed serpentine metal pipes, featuring a manual valve at the bottom. The design is functional, with visible brackets and a solid back panel.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full serpentine pipe arrangement, brackets, and the manual valve at the bottom.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth of the radiator and the protruding pipework from the flat panel.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the plain, solid back panel with no visible features.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the pipework's profile and its attachment to the main panel.
attribute_pipe_connections: single connection at bottom left, horizontal orientation
